_meta.description: League champions (title winners) by season. titlesByManager is authoritative and sums to 16 (one per canonical season). The year-by-year 'byYear' list is INCOMPLETE: only seasons confirmed in the source docs / legacy headers are filled; the rest are pending extraction from each gen_h2h_YYYY.py header (which comments the season's final standings/champion).
_meta.source: STATUS.md (2009), legacy/gen_h2h_2011.py header (2011), legacy/gen_h2h_2024.py header (2024 Daniel), HANDOFF.md (2023, 2025), scraped_2025_2010.md (2010 David), reference/kdp_report.html (2025 + titlesByManager counts)
_meta.seasonCount: RESOLVED by Patrick (2026-07): 2010 IS a canonical season and David won it. So the league has 17 canonical seasons (2009-2025 inclusive). The '16 seasons' repeated throughout the legacy docs/report is a STALE UNDERCOUNT from when 2010 was missing entirely from the project data; it should read 17. Consequence: there are 17 champions/titles total, but the report-derived titlesByManager below still sums to 16 (built before 2010 was added). Per Patrick, David's title is 2010, so titlesByManager already reflects it as David's 1 title. The remaining gap (16 -> 17) will close when the 11 pending champions (2012-2022) are extracted; one of those seasons currently has an uncredited/departed-manager champion. Also update the '16 seasons' references in README/HANDOFF/report when convenient.

byYear.note: Canonical season, confirmed by Patrick (2026-07). Standings-only (no weekly data); see seasons/2010.json.
Confirmed in gen_h2h_2024.py header (Final: Jeremy 142.06 vs Daniel 147.88).

pending.howToFill: Extract each champion from the corresponding legacy/gen_h2h_YYYY.py header comment (final standings). These 11 years hold the remaining 11 titles (Antony x3, duncan x2, Rudee, Larson, RyanC, PatrickF, KyleP, and one more).
